title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
root_mechanism: Insulin resistance → compensatory hyperinsulinaemia → ovarian theca cell stimulation → androgen excess (testosterone, DHEAS) → LH/FSH ratio imbalance → anovulation and follicular cyst formation. Hypothalamic GnRH pulse dysregulation perpetuates the cycle.

interventions.intervention: Sunlight exposure (10 min) + cold shower (2 min)
interventions.physiological_effect: Morning light resets SCN circadian clock → normalises nocturnal LH pulse amplitude; cold lowers elevated androgens via adrenal cortisol modulation

interventions.intervention: Castor oil pelvic Abhyanga (lower abdomen + sacrum area)
interventions.physiological_effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid penetrates ovarian capsule → reduces cyst-associated inflammation; sacral massage stimulates parasympathetic pelvic nerves → improves ovarian blood flow
Balances hypothalamic-pituitary axis via autonomic modulation → normalises GnRH pulsatility → improves LH:FSH ratio

interventions.intervention: Low-GI high-protein: eggs + avocado + flaxseed smoothie
interventions.physiological_effect: Flaxseeds: lignans compete with oestrogen receptors → reduce estrogen dominance; low-GI breakfast lowers fasting insulin surge

interventions.intervention: Hot castor oil pack on lower abdomen (ovarian region)
interventions.physiological_effect: Heat + ricinoleic acid → increases local prostaglandin E1 → promotes follicular maturation; lymphatic stimulation reduces ovarian congestion
Spearmint reduces free testosterone by 30% (documented in RCTs); Shatavari (phytoestrogen adaptogen) normalises FSH and LH

interventions.intervention: Anti-androgen, anti-inflammatory: rainbow salad + legumes + seed cycling
interventions.physiological_effect: Seed cycling: pumpkin + flax seeds (follicular phase), sesame + sunflower (luteal) — provides zinc, selenium, omega-3 to balance hormones phase-specifically
Exercise reduces androgen levels; pelvic floor strengthening improves uterine and ovarian circulation; GLUT4 translocation improves insulin sensitivity

interventions.intervention: Hip bath (warm, with neem or triphala decoction)
interventions.physiological_effect: Warm hip bath dilates pelvic vessels → reduces ovarian congestion and cyst pressure; neem is anti-androgenic
Hip-opening postures increase pelvic blood flow; twisting postures massage ovaries; pranayama regulates cortisol and androgens

interventions.intervention: Dinner: light, anti-inflammatory — cooked vegetables + dal + small portion grain
interventions.physiological_effect: Early light dinner reduces overnight insulin; evening insulin spikes directly stimulate androgen production

interventions.intervention: Warm Epsom salt bath with lavender essential oil
interventions.physiological_effect: Magnesium reduces stress-induced cortisol and DHEAS; lavender reduces anxiety-driven LH dysregulation via limbic system

exit_criteria.target: Reduction in cyst number or size

practices.maintenance_dose: If cycle delays or pelvic pain returns

relapse_rules.action: Phase 2 for 6 weeks + pelvic packs daily

dietary_protocol.eat_window: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM (16:8 IF — reduces overnight insulin and androgen production)
dietary_protocol.meal_order: Protein → Fibre → Low-GI carbs (last and small)

fasting_rules.specification: No eating after 6–7 PM; resume 10–11 AM
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Prolonged overnight fast reduces fasting insulin → reduces ovarian androgen stimulation; activates AMPK → improves insulin receptor sensitivity in ovarian theca cells

fasting_rules.specification: Days 1–14 (follicular): 1 tbsp flax + 1 tbsp pumpkin seeds/day. Days 15–28 (luteal): 1 tbsp sesame + 1 tbsp sunflower seeds/day
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Follicular phase: flax lignans modulate oestrogen; pumpkin zinc supports follicular maturation. Luteal phase: sesame lignans support progesterone; sunflower vitamin E supports corpus luteum function

fasting_rules.specification: 1 cup morning, 1 cup afternoon — steep fresh spearmint 5 min
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Free spearmint oil reduces 5-alpha reductase activity → reduces DHT conversion; documented 30% reduction in free testosterone in PCOS in RCT (30 days)

fasting_rules.specification: Eliminate all white rice, maida, sugar, fruit juices completely
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Every refined carb spike → insulin spike → theca cell stimulation → androgen surge; insulin is the primary driver of PCOS androgen production

fasting_rules.specification: 2 g powder in warm water 15 min before lunch
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Phytoestrogenic saponins (Shatavarin I-IV) normalise FSH and LH by competing at hypothalamic oestrogen receptors; reduces LH:FSH ratio

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Very low-GI: 20–40 g/day net carbs (non-starchy vegetables only; no grains in Phase 1)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate-high: 35–40% (flaxseed, avocado, olive oil — phytoestrogenic and anti-androgenic fats)
phase_macros.rationale: Aggressive insulin reduction in Phase 1; low carb drops fasting insulin → reduces theca cell androgen stimulation directly; high protein + fat maintains satiety without insulin spikes

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Low-GI: 50–80 g/day (introduce millets — ragi, oats — at lunch only)
phase_macros.rationale: Gradual reintroduction of complex carbs; monitor menstrual cycle and fasting insulin response; seed cycling maintained throughout
Lifelong low-GI diet with seed cycling; no refined carbs; spearmint tea continued for anti-androgenic effect; monitor cycle regularity monthly

items: Eggs (daily — zinc + choline for ovarian health)
Fatty fish — salmon, mackerel (omega-3 reduces androgens)
Leafy greens — spinach, kale, fenugreek leaves
Psyllium husk (1 tsp in water — reduces androgen absorption in gut)
Dairy in excess (IGF-1 → stimulates ovarian androgen production)
Red meat in excess (saturated fat → androgen precursors)
Alcohol (increases testosterone by impairing liver oestrogen clearance)
Soy in excess at high doses (phytoestrogen disruption)
Gluten (if gut-associated PCOS — trial elimination for 6 weeks)
Trans fats (worsen insulin resistance and inflammation)

meals.what_to_eat: Spearmint tea (1 cup, fresh leaves, 5 min steep — no sugar) + 2 glasses warm water
meals.physiological_rationale: Spearmint immediately begins anti-androgenic action; warm water maintains fasting state; no calories to avoid insulin spike

meals.what_to_eat: 1 tbsp flaxseeds (Days 1–14) OR sesame seeds (Days 15–28) + apple cider vinegar 2 tbsp in water — still fasting
meals.physiological_rationale: Seeds taken on empty stomach maximise lignan absorption; ACV primes gut for lower insulin response to first meal

meals.what_to_eat: 2–3 eggs (scrambled in coconut oil) + ½ avocado + small salad (cucumber, spinach, lemon) + 1 tsp pumpkin seeds
meals.physiological_rationale: High protein + anti-androgenic fats open eating window; avocado plant sterols reduce androgens; pumpkin seeds zinc supports follicular maturation

meals.what_to_eat: Shatavari decoction (2 g in warm water) — 15 min before eating
meals.physiological_rationale: Shatavarin saponins prepare hypothalamus for FSH normalisation; pre-meal timing allows absorption before food

meals.what_to_eat: Large rainbow salad (spinach, cucumber, bell pepper, beetroot, flaxseed dressing) + moong dal (protein + phytoestrogens) + ½ cup ragi (Phase 2 only — no grains Phase 1)
meals.physiological_rationale: Rainbow vegetables provide anti-oxidant protection for follicles; moong dal phytoestrogens compete with androgens at receptors; ragi low-GI maintains insulin stability

meals.what_to_eat: Spearmint tea (2nd cup) + walnuts (4–5) + 1 tsp cinnamon in warm water
meals.physiological_rationale: 2nd spearmint dose maintains anti-androgenic effect throughout day; walnuts omega-3 reduces ovarian inflammation; cinnamon reduces insulin resistance

meals.what_to_eat: Vegetable soup (no starchy vegetables) + grilled fish or paneer (50 g) + steamed vegetables with turmeric and ginger + small lentil portion
meals.physiological_rationale: Early dinner reduces overnight insulin → reduces overnight androgen production; fish omega-3 reduces LH-driven androgen surge; turmeric reduces ovarian cyst inflammation

meals.what_to_eat: 16-hour fast begins — spearmint tea allowed; no food until 10 AM next day
meals.physiological_rationale: Overnight insulin suppression is the primary therapeutic lever in PCOS; 16-h fast reduces androgen production by 25–40% in studies

functional_foods.name: Spearmint tea
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 cups/day — morning and afternoon; steep fresh spearmint 5 min, no sugar
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Free spearmint oil inhibits 5-alpha reductase → reduces DHT; reduces luteinising hormone (LH); documented 30% reduction in free testosterone in PCOS in RCT (Phytotherapy Research, 2010)

functional_foods.name: Flaxseeds (follicular phase)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tbsp ground — Days 1–14 of menstrual cycle daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Secoisolariciresinol diglucoside (SDG) lignans bind oestrogen receptors → modulate oestrogen dominance; reduce sex hormone binding globulin (SHBG) depression → reduce free androgen index

functional_foods.name: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 2 g powder in warm water — before lunch, supervised (3 months on, 1 month off)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Shatavarin I–IV saponins act as phytoestrogenic adaptogens; normalise FSH and LH by competing at hypothalamic oestrogen receptors; reduce LH:FSH ratio in PCOS

functional_foods.name: Cinnamon
functional_foods.dose_timing: ½ tsp in warm water or food — daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: MHCP improves insulin receptor signalling; documented to restore menstrual cyclicity in 4/15 PCOS women in 8 weeks (pilot RCT); reduces fasting insulin and HOMA-IR

functional_foods.name: Pumpkin seeds (follicular phase)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Rich in zinc — essential for follicular development and LH receptor function; zinc deficiency worsens PCOS androgen excess; also provides anti-androgenic plant sterols

functional_foods.name: Sesame seeds (luteal phase)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Sesamin lignans support progesterone production in corpus luteum; lignans modulate androgen receptor sensitivity; omega-6/omega-3 balance supports luteal phase adequacy

functional_foods.name: Ashwagandha
functional_foods.dose_timing: 300 mg standardised extract — before bed, supervised (3 months on, 1 month off)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Reduces cortisol 27% (RCT) → reduces stress-driven DHEAS elevation in PCOS; withanolides normalise HPO-HPA axis crosstalk; improves thyroid function (commonly co-morbid in PCOS)

functional_foods.name: Castor oil (pelvic pack)
functional_foods.dose_timing: Warm pack on lower abdomen — 25 min, daily Phase 1 / 4×/week Phase 2
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Ricinoleic acid penetrates ovarian capsule → reduces prostaglandin-mediated ovarian inflammation; lymphatic stimulation reduces ovarian congestion; documented to increase pelvic circulation in observational studies

asanas.physiological_effect: Hip opening → increases pelvic blood flow; stretches ovarian ligaments reducing adhesion and congestion around follicles
Passive hip opening; reduces ovarian congestion; stimulates parasympathetic pelvic nerves → improves follicular blood flow
Compresses pelvic floor → massages ovaries and uterus mechanically; stimulates pelvic floor reflex zones; opens sacral parasympathetic outflow
Improves venous drainage from pelvis; reduces ovarian cystic pressure; reverses gravitational stress on pelvic organs
Massages ovaries + adrenals simultaneously; reduces androgen excess from adrenal source (DHEAS); compresses and releases ovarian capsule
Hypothalamic-pituitary axis regulation via ANS balancing; normalises GnRH pulsatility → improves LH:FSH ratio
Cortisol reduction → reduces DHEAS (adrenal androgen); HPA-HPO axis resetting; allows pelvic floor complete relaxation

red_flags.action: Rule out premature ovarian failure or secondary amenorrhoea — urgent gynaecology
Rule out ovarian torsion — surgical emergency
Rule out ovarian/adrenal tumour; urgent endocrinology referral
Collaborate with reproductive endocrinologist before protocol intensification
PCOS-associated T2DM — add diabetes protocol concurrently

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed to be implemented under the supervision of a qualified BNYS physician. Patients desiring fertility must consult a reproductive endocrinologist. Shatavari and ashwagandha require clinician supervision. This document is for clinical reference only and does not substitute for individualised medical assessment.
